feedback.text: Hello, good afternoon. I was led here to this feedback as I am following something of interest to me. The initiative "Stop Killing Games". I'm 35 as of now, and one of my hobbies is gaming, it has been since I was 5 years old. As you can imagine I've spent a lot of money on consoles, PC's, hardware linked to the previous items, and A LOT of money in buying games. I don't think it's fair that I've bought so many games and I don't have control over them. Can you imagine booting the console or PC to play something I've bought and not being able to play, asking for a refund and being shut down, and loosing all my hard worked money? I actually think this is mal-practice and hurtfull to the customer by the companies. One thing is, if I you tell me that from this day forward these practice will be the industry standard, then marketing, pricing and all related to videogame sales needs to be recaped. I don't want to spend 70 to play a game for a month. If I'm "leasing" a license to play, then I want a different sale strategy. But, I'm hopuing it doesn't come to this. Or else, I might be out of a hobby. I urge you to help us in this regard and protect the customer, in this case, the gamers. And if not, at least to make the playing field less shadier. Thank you for your time and consideration. Best Regards Diogo Gomes
